The Tajaka method of solar revolution does work
very well; and some method for rectifying the given time of
birth can be elucidated from this. I propose to do it on a
subsequent occasion. There are many methods which are
similar to this one and they are to a certain extent fruitful.
The prgressive solar revolution method c6risiders one year
and one day to be equivalent to one year of life. Naibad's
method takes the Sun's mean daily motion of 58'8" as equiva
lent to one year oflife. But the Simmonite's method considers
the Sun's actual daily motion after birth to represent one year
of birth.
Apart from all these, I wish to draw the attention of the
readers to a method I came across in an astrological treatise in
Sanskrit. It asks us to treat one day prior to the birthday as
equivalent to one year. Thus for one born on 19th September,
you have to look for the planetary positions and cusps on 30th
